meta.name: Kelewele
meta.subtitle: Ghanaian spicy fried plantains with ginger, chili, and warm spices

source.name: ssa
notes: Classic Ghana street-food style kelewele: ripe plantains seasoned with ginger, chili, and warm spices, then fried until caramelized.
Designed for SSA: clear signals, scalable heat, and equipment-minimum friendly.

summary: Ripe plantain cubes tossed in a ginger-chili spice mix and fried until crisp-edged and caramelized—sweet, spicy, and fragrant.

yield.notes: Snack-size portions. Makes about 3–4 cups fried plantain cubes.

signals.makeAhead: Season plantains up to 2 hours ahead; fry just before serving.

ingredients.label: Ripe plantains
notes: Best when peel is mostly yellow with heavy black spotting (ripe, sweet).
Avoid fully soft/mushy plantains—they can break apart while frying.

ingredients.label: Fresh ginger, finely grated
ingredients.notes: About a 2-inch knob. Adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Garlic, finely grated or minced
ingredients.notes: Optional but common in modern versions.

ingredients.label: Ground dried chili or cayenne
ingredients.notes: Use 1/4 tsp for mild; 1 tsp+ for hot.

ingredients.label: Ground nutmeg
ingredients.notes: Kelewele signature note—don’t skip if possible.

ingredients.label: Ground cloves
ingredients.notes: A pinch; cloves can dominate quickly.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Start here; adjust after frying if needed.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil for frying (peanut/canola/vegetable)
ingredients.notes: Enough for 3/4–1 inch depth in a pan (amount depends on pan size).

ingredients.label: Roasted peanuts (optional serving)
ingredients.notes: Traditional street-style pairing. Omit if avoiding nuts.

steps.title: Peel and cut the plantains
instructions: Trim both ends of each plantain. Score the peel lengthwise and remove peel.
Place cubes in a mixing bowl.
checks: Plantains are evenly cut so they fry at the same rate.

steps.title: Make the kelewele seasoning
instructions: In a small bowl, combine grated ginger (and garlic if using), chili/cayenne, black pepper, nutmeg, optional cloves, and salt.
Add 1–2 teaspoons of water to form a loose paste (optional but helps distribute seasoning).
checks: Seasoning is fragrant and evenly mixed.

steps.title: Coat and rest briefly
instructions: Add seasoning paste to the plantain cubes and toss until evenly coated.
Let rest 10 minutes so flavors cling and soak in slightly.
checks: Plantains look evenly coated with spice paste.

steps.title: Heat the oil
instructions: Add oil to a heavy pan to a depth of about 3/4 to 1 inch.
If you don’t have a thermometer, test with one plantain cube: it should sizzle actively and rise to the surface within a few seconds.

temperature.notes: Maintain 350–365°F for best caramelization without burning spices.

checks: Oil is hot enough for steady sizzling but not smoking.

steps.title: Fry in batches until caramelized
instructions: Fry plantains in small batches (don’t crowd the pan).
Stir gently so pieces don’t stick and cook evenly.
Fry 4–6 minutes per batch until deep golden with caramelized edges and slightly crisp exterior.
checks: Pieces are deep golden with crisp edges; spices smell toasted, not burnt.

steps.title: Drain and serve
instructions: Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels or a wire rack.
Taste and add a pinch more salt if needed.
Serve hot with optional sliced red onion and roasted peanuts.
checks: Seasoning is balanced—sweet, spicy, and aromatic.

pairings.notes: Often served as a snack on its own or alongside spicy sauces and grilled meats/fish.

fridge.notes: Best fresh. Reheat to restore crisp edges.

reheat.notes: Reheat in an air fryer at 375°F for 4–6 minutes or in a hot skillet with a tiny splash of oil.

notes: Hot oil can splatter—dry plantains well if rinsed and use caution.
Spices can burn if oil is too hot; keep oil under 375°F and fry in batches.
Oil depth matters: too shallow can scorch spices before the plantain caramelizes; aim for at least 3/4-inch.
If plantains brown too fast, reduce heat slightly and fry smaller batches.
For a softer inside with crisp edges, choose ripe plantains that are sweet but still firm.

adapt.notes: Toss seasoned plantains with 1–2 tbsp oil, air-fry at 390°F for 10–14 minutes, shaking halfway. Texture differs (less glossy caramelization) but still tasty.
Reduce cayenne to 1/8 tsp or omit; keep ginger + nutmeg for signature flavor. Serve with a squeeze of lime if desired.
Increase cayenne to 1–1.5 tsp and add a minced fresh hot pepper to the seasoning paste.
